description: Sample pile responses. Quantities for sampling include force and moment at one end of the pile, axial stress for elements with a plastic material model and displacement, stress, and yield state in both the normal and shear coupling springs. The force and moment are expressed using the force-moment sign convention .

6.0.description: Sample pile responses. Quantities for sampling include force and moment acting on the pile itself, and displacement, stress, and yield state in both the normal and shear coupling springs.

keywords.name: coupling-displacement-normal
keywords.description: displacement in the normal direction coupling spring at end of the element. Sign convention for normal spring is+/-, indicating separation/overlap.

keywords.name: coupling-stress-normal
keywords.description: stress in the normal direction coupling spring at end of the element. Sign convention for normal spring is +/-, indicating separation/ overlap.

keywords.name: coupling-yield-normal
keywords.description: yield state in the normal direction coupling spring at end of the element. Value {0, 1, 2} denotes never yielded, yielding now, or yielded in past, respectively.

keywords.name: coupling-displacement-shear
keywords.description: displacement in the shear direction coupling spring at end of the element. Sign convention for shear spring is +/- with respect to average axial direction of pile.

keywords.name: coupling-stress-shear
keywords.description: stress in the shear direction coupling spring at end of the element. Sign convention for shear spring is +/- with respect to average axial direction of pile.

keywords.name: coupling-yield-shear
keywords.description: yield state in the shear coupling spring at end of the element. Value {0, 1, 2} denotes never yielded, yielding now, or yielded in past, respectively.

keywords.name: force
keywords.description: force magnitude

keywords.name: force-x
keywords.description: force (x-component, element system)

keywords.name: force-y
keywords.description: force (y-component, element system)

keywords.name: force-z
keywords.description: force (z-component, element system)

keywords.name: moment
keywords.description: moment magnitude

keywords.name: moment-x
keywords.description: moment (x-component, element system)

keywords.name: moment-y
keywords.description: moment (y-component, element system)

keywords.name: moment-z
keywords.description: moment (z-component, element system)

keywords.name: component-id
keywords.description: the particular element is identified by the CID number. If this keyword is used, position should not be used.

keywords.name: end
keywords.description: the quantity will be sampled at end i (where i is 1 or 2), which corresponds with the order in the nodal-connectivity list. The history is sampled at end 1 (the default) if this keyword is not supplied.

keywords.name: position
keywords.description: the particular element is identified by (v) coordinates (the nearest element is taken). If this keyword is used, component-id should not be used.

keywords.description: force ( \(x\) -component, force-moment sign convention)

keywords.description: force ( \(z\) -component, force-moment sign convention)

keywords.name: ipstress
keywords.description: axial stress ( \(\sigma_{xx}\) ) for an element with a plastic material model. Uses the integration-point keyword.

keywords.name: moment
keywords.description: moment magnitude

keywords.name: moment-x
keywords.description: moment ( \(x\) -component, force-moment sign convention)

keywords.name: moment-y
keywords.description: moment ( \(y\) -component, force-moment sign convention)

keywords.name: moment-z
keywords.description: moment ( \(z\) -component, force-moment sign convention)

keywords.name: name
keywords.description: assign a name s to the history record (used to reference the history elsewhere)

keywords.name: component-id
keywords.description: the particular element is identified by the CID number. If this keyword is used, position should not be used.

keywords.name: end
keywords.description: the quantity will be sampled at end i (where i is 1 or 2 ), which corresponds with the order in the nodal-connectivity list. The history is sampled at end 1 (the default) if this keyword is not supplied.

keywords.name: integration-point
keywords.description: the integration point at which the axial stress is sampled. The integration point locations are shown here and here . If this keyword is not specified, then the integration point index is set to 1 . Only available if noted.

keywords.name: position
keywords.description: the particular element is identified by ( v ) coordinates (the nearest element is taken). If this keyword is used, component-id should not be used.
